refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; implement predictive load balancing; minimize idle states.
DP-B: Implement real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive control feedback; use telemetry to detect and compensate for duty cycle variance.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services; enforce hard timing constraints via scheduling and prioritization.
DP-D: Combine vehicle optimization (DP-A), real-time monitoring (DP-B), and budget allocation (DP-C) with safety-aware scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a multi-layer cycle time management strategy: (1) optimize vehicle subsystem performance within safety envelope; (2) deploy real-time telemetry-based cycle time monitoring and adaptive feedback; (3) allocate and enforce cycle time budgets across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services via priority-aware scheduling that preserves safety-critical response.

potential_sources: Vehicle optimization (DP-A) must not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response (coupled to Safety Services FR).
Teleoperation latency budget (DP-B) is coupled to network link quality and tele-operator skill (external dependencies).
Safety-aware scheduling (DP-C) couples cycle time performance to safety criticality; safety interventions may legitimately exceed cycle time budget.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite strategy that acknowledges coupling between performance and safety. Decoupling is achieved by explicit budget allocation and priority rules: safety-critical actions are never sacrificed for cycle time. This is acceptable given high safety criticality tailoring.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle subsystems can be optimized to meet cycle time target without design iteration.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure is available and reliable for real-time monitoring.
Assumption: Tele-operator skill and network latency are within acceptable bounds (external to NGHE design).
Risk: Adaptive feedback control may introduce instability if tuning is poor; requires validation testing.
Risk: Safety-aware scheduling complexity may introduce latency in non-safety-critical paths; requires careful priority design.
Risk: Duty cycle variance at customer site may exceed design envelope; requires field characterization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be refined to explicitly separate vehicle performance optimization (DP-A, owned by Vehicle module) from teleoperation and safety scheduling (DP-B/C, owned by Teleoperation and Safety Services). This clarifies ownership and reduces coupling at the IM level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level cycle time orchestration and performance management. Owns the integration of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services cycle time budgets; owns telemetry aggregation and reporting; owns adaptive feedback control logic that coordinates across subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load time, travel time, dump time, total cycle time, p95 statistics) to external monitoring/reporting systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definition and CT_target from Site Manager or planning system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety intervention signals from Safety Services and may adjust cycle time budget allocation in response; sends cycle time performance data to Safety Services for correlation analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates with Teleoperation module via network link; monitors latency and adjusts cycle time budget allocation if network conditions degrade.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of cycle time orchestration, budget allocation, and telemetry aggregation. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry emission and tasking reception cross boundaries.

Vehicle owns vehicle subsystem optimization (DP-A) but does not own teleoperation or safety scheduling logic. Cycle time monitoring and adaptive feedback require cross-module coordination; high diffusion if Vehicle tries to own full DP-D.

Teleoperation owns latency budget and control overhead (DP-B component) but does not own vehicle optimization or safety scheduling. Cycle time orchestration is a system-level concern; high diffusion if Teleoperation owns full DP-D.

Safety Services owns safety-aware scheduling constraints (DP-C component) but does not own vehicle optimization or teleoperation latency. Cycle time orchestration is a system-level concern; high diffusion if Safety Services owns full DP-D.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution owns the cycle time management strategy (DP-D) as a system-level orchestration layer. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services implement their respective sub-strategies (DP-A, DP-B, DP-C) and expose cycle time performance metrics via Telemetry interface. NGHE Solution aggregates these metrics, applies adaptive feedback control, and enforces budget allocation via priority rules.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a thin orchestration layer without clear ownership of adaptive feedback logic, responsibility for cycle time performance may be ambiguous. Mitigation: Explicitly define adaptive feedback control algorithms and their ownership within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if cycle time monitoring is too frequent or verbose. Mitigation: Define telemetry sampling rates and aggregation windows to balance monitoring fidelity and overhead.

Risk: Safety Services may override cycle time budget allocation during safety interventions, causing p95 cycle time to exceed target. Mitigation: Explicitly document that safety-critical interventions are exempt from cycle time targets; define acceptance criteria that account for safety event frequency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the ownership of adaptive feedback control logic within NGHE Solution. Define the telemetry schema and sampling rates to ensure monitoring overhead does not degrade cycle time. Document the priority rules that govern cycle time budget allocation when safety interventions occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (multi-layer cycle time management) directly addresses FR-NGHE-002 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The strategy is comprehensive: vehicle optimization, real-time monitoring, and budget allocation are all necessary to meet the KPI. No independence violations detected; the three sub-strategies (DP-A, DP-B, DP-C) are orthogonal and collectively sufficient.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Adaptive feedback control logic may be distributed across NGHE Solution, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if ownership is not clearly defined. Mitigation: Assign adaptive feedback control to NGHE Solution; other modules expose metrics only.
Diffusion risk: Telemetry aggregation may be duplicated if multiple modules independently monitor cycle time. Mitigation: Designate NGHE Solution as the single source of truth for cycle time metrics.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for DP-D. Containment score of 0.95 indicates that the strategy is well-localized within the system-level module. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are minimal and well-defined. No significant diffusion expected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials. Cycle time metrics can be collected via Telemetry interface and analyzed to compute p95 statistics. Safety event correlation can be performed to ensure no safety compromise. Duty cycle variance can be characterized and compared against design envelope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns system-level cycle time orchestration; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own sub-component strategies and expose metrics via Telemetry. Ownership boundaries are well-defined and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to explicitly separate vehicle optimization (DP-A, owned by Vehicle) from teleoperation and safety scheduling (DP-B/C, owned by Teleoperation and Safety Services). NGHE Solution owns orchestration and adaptive feedback.
Define the telemetry schema for cycle time metrics: load time, travel time, dump time, total cycle time, p95 statistics, safety event flags.
Document the priority rules that govern cycle time budget allocation when safety interventions occur. Explicitly state that safety-critical actions are never sacrificed for cycle time.
Define acceptance criteria that account for safety event frequency and duty cycle variance. Specify how p95 cycle time is computed (e.g., over what time window, under what conditions).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs for vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through FR-NGHE-002.4) require further decomposition to allocate cycle time budgets and define performance targets for each subsystem. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership and enable detailed design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems can be optimized to meet cycle time target without design iteration.
Telemetry infrastructure is available and reliable for real-time monitoring.
Tele-operator skill and network latency are within acceptable bounds (external to NGHE design).
Duty cycle is well-characterized and stable across customer sites.
Safety-critical interventions are acceptable exceptions to cycle time targets.
open_risks: Adaptive feedback control may introduce instability if tuning is poor; requires validation testing.
Safety-aware scheduling complexity may introduce latency in non-safety-critical paths; requires careful priority design.
Duty cycle variance at customer site may exceed design envelope; requires field characterization.
Telemetry overhead may degrade cycle time if monitoring is too frequent; requires careful sampling rate design.
Ambiguity in adaptive feedback control ownership may lead to diffusion across modules; requires explicit assignment.
evidence_needed: Vehicle subsystem performance data (load time, travel time, dump time) under representative duty cycles.
Teleoperation latency and control overhead measurements under various network conditions.
Safety Services response time and blocking delay analysis.
Field trial data showing p95 cycle time distribution and correlation with safety events.
Duty cycle characterization from customer sites (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill).
